name: Mouth of the Tajuña River — circular via the Camino del Cid from Anguita
name_original: Hoz del río Tajuña — circular por Camino del Cid desde Anguita
region_intro: Hiking around Anguita offers diverse landscapes within the Alto Tajo Natural Park, characterized by varied geology and the presence of the Tajuña River. The region provides accessible trails that cater to different fitness levels, from gentle village loops to more challenging routes with significant elevation changes. Hikers can explore areas featuring river valleys, natural rock formations, and the unique terrain of the natural park. Best hiking trails around Anguita The most popular hiking route is Cueva de los Casares and Valley of Miracles Loop, an 8.0 miles (12.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 57 minutes to complete. This route showcases diverse geology and is considered moderate in difficulty. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mouth of the Tajuña River — circular via the Camino del Cid from Anguita, a moderate 9.4 miles (15.2 km) path. This trail leads to the Mouth of the Tajuña River and requires good fitness. Local hikers also love the Anguita Village and Church loop from Anguita, a 7.1 miles (11.5 km) trail leading through the village and its surroundings, often completed in about 3 hours 6 minutes. Hiking around Anguita is defined by the Alto Tajo Natural Park, the Tajuña River, and varied geological features. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Anguita are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 15 hikers have used komoot to explore Anguita's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Anguita?
A: There are 9 hiking routes around Anguita, offering a variety of experiences within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. These trails cater to different fitness levels, from easy strolls to more challenging excursions.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners in Anguita?
A: Yes, Anguita offers several easy hiking options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24540957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Anguita&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 7.6 km trail. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38147539" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anguita Village and Church loop from Anguita&lt;/a&gt; is also a good option, providing accessible paths around the village.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes around Anguita?
A: Hiking around Anguita means exploring the diverse landscapes of the Alto Tajo Natural Park. You'll encounter varied geology, river valleys, and natural rock formations. Notable features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Casares&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valley of Miracles&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4320256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajuña River Gorges&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Anguita?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Anguita are circular. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030415843/mouth-of-the-tajuna-river-circular-via-the-camino-del-cid-from-anguita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mouth of the Tajuña River — circular via the Camino del Cid from Anguita&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 15.2 km path. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030461685/cueva-de-los-casares-and-valley-of-miracles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Casares and Valley of Miracles Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.0-mile (12.9 km) route.

Q: What are some challenging hikes available near Anguita?
A: While most routes are moderate, some sections of trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030461685/cueva-de-los-casares-and-valley-of-miracles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Casares and Valley of Miracles Loop&lt;/a&gt; can involve significant elevation gain (up to 3,000 ft) and require good fitness. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030415843/mouth-of-the-tajuna-river-circular-via-the-camino-del-cid-from-anguita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mouth of the Tajuña River — circular via the Camino del Cid from Anguita&lt;/a&gt; is also considered a moderate hike requiring good fitness.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Anguita?
A: Anguita offers hiking opportunities throughout the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, ideal for longer hikes. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended. Winter offers a different, often quieter, experience, though some higher elevation paths might require appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest to explore along the trails?
A: Yes, you can explore several interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4272783" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anguita Village and Church&lt;/a&gt; is a central point for some loops. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Casares&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valley of Miracles&lt;/a&gt;, which are significant natural and geological sites.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Anguita?
A: Information on specific public transport routes directly to all trailheads is limited. It is generally advisable to plan for personal vehicle use to access the more remote starting points for hikes around Anguita and within the Alto Tajo Natural Park.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking in Anguita?
A: Parking is typically available within Anguita village itself, which serves as a common starting point for several routes, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38147539" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anguita Village and Church loop&lt;/a&gt;. For other trailheads, roadside parking might be available, but it's best to check specific route details before you go.

Q: Do I need any permits to hike in Alto Tajo Natural Park near Anguita?
A: Generally, permits are not required for standard hiking activities on marked trails within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. However, for specific activities or access to certain protected areas, it's always recommended to check the official park regulations or local information sources before your visit.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Anguita?
A: The hiking routes in Anguita are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes within the Alto Tajo Natural Park, the varied geological features, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly hiking trails around Anguita?
A: Many trails in the Anguita area and within the Alto Tajo Natural Park are generally dog-friendly, provided dogs are kept under control, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife. It's always a good idea to bring water for your dog and check specific trail regulations for any restrictions before heading out.
tips: Town of the homonymous municipality in the province of Guadalajara, located on the edge of one of the gorges of the Tajuña River.

The gorges of the Tajuña River form a landscape of great beauty between the towns of Anguita and Luzón. Along about 11 kilometres, the passage of the river has created curious cracks and caves in the rock. If you are doing the Camino del Cid, it is worth deviating from the road and venturing down a dirt track to enjoy this environment.
